Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order/To Strike Experts
Off Calendar Oct-28-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Jennifer Sommer, Atty. for defendant and moving party: Joseph Schoepp, Julianne Schoepp
Phil & David Weltin, Atty. for Plaintiff and Opposing party: Matos
Ian Zimmerman, Atty. for Plaintiff and Opposing party: Mirtha Estrada

RULING: HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Defendants Joseph A. Schoepp's And Julianne S. Schoepp's Motion For Protective Order/To Strike Experts is denied in its entirety as to Plaintiff Estrada. The court finds that Donald Mayo is being proffered by Plaintiff to express an opinion on issues included in the subject matter covered by Scott Buske.

The motion is granted as to Plaintiff Matos. Plaintiff Matos' failure to designate an expert on an issue that requires expert opinion in the initial disclosure bars his purported supplemental disclosure as it does not meet the requirement of a simultaneous designation. Plaintiff Matos did not make any disclosure of a liability expert.

This court does not determine the outcome of any motion at trial made pursuant to C.C.P. Section 2034.310.

The court denies the motion to strike allegedly duplicative liability experts.
COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.
